close_reason: Self-cancelled in review pass: my own description concluded 'not really a finding'. Filing this would be bikeshedding per /review-rusty-crate rules. Marking won't-fix to keep audit trace.
description:
lib.rs:121 declares fn from_uri(uri: &str) -> Result<Self, SecretError>. Both inventory factories (lib.rs:329-333 and 337-341) immediately wrap the result in std::sync::Arc::new(b) and cast to a trait object.

Direct callers (the doctest at lib.rs:64 and tests) use the by-value form, which is correct; the factory wrapping is forced by the registry contract. Not really a finding — Self is correct for direct construction, and the Arc wrap belongs in the registry adapter.

A reviewer might suggest 'just return Arc<Self>' to save the factory the work; this would be wrong because direct callers don't need the Arc and would have to deref through it.

Filing as P4/opinion to capture the design rationale rather than as a fix.
title: from_uri returns Self by-value but inventory factory wraps in Arc — consider returning Arc directly
